items.explanation: 5-14-1 states STARS may be used for identifying aircraft assigned a discrete beacon code, maintaining identity of targets, and performing handoffs between controllers.

choices.text: Identifying aircraft with discrete beacon codes, maintaining target identity, and performing handoffs

items.question_text: Does STARS equipment relieve the controller of responsibility for aircraft identification and separation?
items.explanation: 5-14-2 explicitly states the equipment does NOT relieve the controller of responsibility for proper identification, maintenance of identity, handoff of the correct target, and separation of aircraft.

choices.text: No, the controller remains responsible for identification, identity maintenance, correct handoff, and separation
Yes, STARS automates all controller responsibilities
Only for separation but not identification

items.question_text: What functions may terminal automation be used for?
items.explanation: 5-14-3 lists: tracking, tagging, handoff, altitude information, coordination, ground speed, and identification.

choices.text: Tracking, tagging, handoff, altitude info, coordination, ground speed, and identification
Only altitude information and ground speed

items.question_text: What must be done before terminating use of the terminal automation system at a control position?
items.explanation: 5-14-4a requires informing all appropriate positions before terminating or reinstating use, and transferring or terminating all pertinent flight data.

choices.text: Inform all appropriate positions and transfer/terminate all pertinent flight data

items.question_text: What must be kept current at all times when displayed in STARS?
items.explanation: 5-14-4d requires assigned altitude, if displayed, to be kept current at all times. Climb and descent arrows must be used for other than level flight.

items.question_text: In FUSION mode, when must assigned or pilot reported altitude be displayed?
items.explanation: 5-14-4e requires the assigned or pilot reported altitude to be displayed and kept current when the aircraft is in level flight.

choices.text: When the aircraft is in level flight
At all times regardless of flight status
Only when requested by the pilot

items.question_text: What designators must be used for aircraft identification in STARS unless program limitations dictate otherwise?
items.explanation: 5-14-5a requires two-letter ICAO designators or three-letter designators as appropriate.

items.question_text: When may the inhibit function be used for CA/MCI alerts?
items.explanation: 5-14-6c2 states the inhibit function must only be used for aircraft routinely engaged in operations where approved separation criteria do not apply, such as ADC practice intercepts and air shows.

choices.text: Only for aircraft in operations where approved separation criteria do not apply (e.g., practice intercepts, air shows)
Whenever the controller is too busy
For all aircraft on instrument approaches

items.question_text: For which aircraft must MSAW processing be inhibited?
items.explanation: 5-14-7a requires inhibiting MSAW processing of VFR aircraft and aircraft that cancel IFR flight plans unless the pilot specifically requests otherwise.

choices.text: VFR aircraft and aircraft that cancel IFR flight plans

items.question_text: When should the track suspend function be used?
items.explanation: 5-14-8 permits track suspend only when data block overlap in holding patterns or final approach proximity creates an unworkable situation. Suspend non-altitude readout tracks first.

choices.text: Only when data block overlap in holding patterns or near final approach creates an unworkable situation
Whenever the controller wants to reduce clutter

items.question_text: What action must be taken when an ARV (Approach Runway Verification) alert is received?
items.explanation: 5-14-9a requires evaluating the reason for the alert and taking appropriate action as needed.

choices.text: Evaluate the reason for the alert and take appropriate action

items.question_text: May ARV alerts be suppressed for another control position without coordination?
items.explanation: 5-14-9d states ARV alerts may NOT be suppressed for another control position without being coordinated or as specified in a facility directive.

choices.text: No, coordination or facility directive is required
Yes, if the controller is too busy

items.instruction: What do you need to ensure?
items.situation: You are working a STARS position and need to terminate automation to do a system reset. Other controllers are working adjacent positions.

choices.text: Inform all appropriate positions and ensure all pertinent flight data is transferred or terminated
Simply initiate the reset without notification
Have all aircraft change to another frequency

items.explanation: 5-14-4A requires informing all appropriate positions before terminating and transferring or terminating all pertinent flight data.

items.instruction: In what order should tracks be suspended?
items.situation: Data block overlap in a holding pattern has created an unworkable situation on your STARS display. Some tracks display automatic altitude readouts and some do not.
items.question_text: Which tracks should be suspended first?

choices.text: Those not displaying automatic altitude readouts first; then those with altitude readouts if needed
Those displaying automatic altitude readouts first

items.explanation: 5-14-8: Suspend tracks without altitude readouts first. Only if condition still exists, suspend those with altitude readouts.
